title: fix(braintrust): use resolved model ID for accurate cost estimation
summary: When using AI gateways (e.g. Vercel AI Gateway), the Braintrust exporter sets metadata.model to the gateway alias (e.g. 'claude-sonnet-4.5') instead of the resolved provider model ID (e.g. 'claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929'). Braintrust can't match aliases to its pricing database, so cost estimation fails. This PR prefers the already-available `responseModel` attribute (populated from `response.modelId`) over the request-time `model` value, and adds `responseModel` to the `omitKeys` list to avoid duplication in metadata.
maintainerNotes: Very clean, minimal fix. The `responseModel` attribute is well-established in the codebase — it's already used by the Laminar and OTel exporters for the same purpose (see laminar/src/tracing.ts:520, otel-exporter/src/gen-ai-semantics.ts:261). The type `ModelGenerationAttributes.responseModel` is defined in core (observability/types/tracing.ts:186) and populated from `response.modelId` in the model loop. No tests added but the change is straightforward conditional assignment. Missing a changeset. CI checks are stuck in pending state (likely needs secrets authorization for the external contributor fork). CodeRabbit approved with one optional nitpick about `provider` not being in the omitKeys list (pre-existing issue).
